avoid: Hardcoding hidden dimensions or tensor sizes.
Replacing cuBLAS for the full W@X GEMM, as it is unlikely to beat cuBLAS.
Overcomplicating the kernel with excessive shared memory usage that increases register pressure without clear benefit.
evidence: Aggregate speedup is 0.997, essentially no improvement over baseline.
Speedup for d=3584 is 1.013, but for d=4096 and 4608 it regresses to 0.994 and 0.986 respectively.
Profile summary identifies bottleneck as rank16_update_or_memory_bandwidth.
Candidate uses scalar access (vector_width=1) and block_size=128, limiting memory throughput.
Correctness is perfect (max_abs_err=0.0), so performance is the only concern.
recommended_actions: Use vectorized loads/stores (e.g., float4) to improve memory bandwidth utilization.
Experiment with different block sizes (e.g., 64, 256) to find optimal occupancy.
Consider using shared memory to cache tiles of X or reduce redundant global reads.
Profile memory throughput to confirm bandwidth saturation and guide optimization.
